libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Generated tests should avoid depending on a real JPA provider unless explicitly testing provider integration; local PersistenceProviderResolver/PersistenceProvider stubs are sufficient. For 4.0.0-M5, update Query and TypedQuery test doubles to implement the new setParameters(Object...) API.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8596 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.persistence:jakarta.persistence-api:4.0.0-M5; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: End-to-end persistence with a real EntityManager would require an implementation such as Hibernate or EclipseLink and likely JDBC/database setup, but that is not necessary for API reachability coverage and would broaden the test scope.
Because the compile failure is caused by new abstract API methods, stale hand-written stubs may fail javac even though no extra dependency or Docker setup is missing.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jakarta.persistence-api is an API-only artifact; its resolved POM only adds jakarta.annotation-api transitively, and meaningful coverage can be reached by exercising annotations, API defaults, exceptions, Persistence bootstrap behavior, and SPI with local test doubles rather than a real provider or database.
